description: Harassing the Ring-billed gulls. Large, larger then the ring-billed gulls, brown over all, streaked underparts, white patch above tail visible in flight (not in photos to well however). Dark bluish beak and legs. Dark coverts with one white wing flash, unlike Pomarine. Under belly not as dark as Pomarine, darker then Long-tailed. Beak is short and thin with no real obvious hook, unlike Long-tailed's large heavy bill with a strong hook look, not as thick beak as Long-tailed either. No long central tail feather ever seen like long-tailed juvenile. Warm coloring to face, more cinnamon colored then Pomarine, not as much white underneath like Long-tailed. Flight in pursuit of ring-billeds was fast and rapid, bringing to mind a falcon for me, but much too big for a falcon and not the right body shape. Ring-billed gulls being chased were giving off alarm calls, more frantic then I have heard before and more constant. This is what alerted me to something different. The gulls were highly agitated this day, rather than loafing around like they do on the shore they would suddenly take to the air at the appearance of this jaeger and circle around, land and repeat as needed. Two terns were flying high overhead, constantly giving an alarm call.
photographer: Photographer: Brian Johnson, Brian Johnson, Brian Johnson, Brian Johnson
